labels.name: extension-request
labels.description: Request to add a new extension

labels.name: new-category
labels.description: Suggests adding a whole new category

labels.name: wontfix

issues.title: [CATEGORY] Add Rust & Systems category
issues.body:
## New Category: Rust & Systems

Rust is the most loved language on Stack Overflow for 8 years. Many devs are picking it up and need a quick VS Code setup.

## Extensions to include

| Name | ID | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Rust Analyzer | `rust-lang.rust-analyzer` | Official Rust language server |
| Crates | `serayuzgur.crates` | Shows crate versions inline in Cargo.toml |
| Even Better TOML | `tamasfe.even-better-toml` | Full TOML syntax support |
| CodeLLDB | `vadimcn.vscode-lldb` | Native debugger for Rust using LLDB |

## How to contribute

1. Fork the repo
2. Create `data/categories/rust.json` using the structure in CONTRIBUTING.md
3. Add `"rust"` to `data/categories/index.json`
4. Run `npm test`
5. Open a PR

issues.title: [BUG] "code" CLI error message needs verification on Windows 10/11
issues.body:
## Bug: Windows error message not verified

On Windows, when `code` is not in PATH, we show instructions but nobody has confirmed they work on real Windows 10/11.

## How to verify and fix

1. In `src/installer.js`, find `getCodeInstallInstructions()`
2. On a Windows machine, follow the instructions step by step
3. If the steps are wrong or the UI has changed, update the message
4. Open a PR with the fix

## Difficulty

Easy. Just verify and update a string.

issues.title: [BUG] Crash when index.json references a category file that does not exist
issues.body:
## Bug: Unhelpful crash on missing category file

If someone adds an ID to `data/categories/index.json` without creating the matching file, the app crashes with a raw Node.js ENOENT error.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Add `"rust"` to `data/categories/index.json`
2. Do NOT create `data/categories/rust.json`
3. Run `node index.js`

## Expected error

```
Error: Category file not found: data/categories/rust.json
Did you forget to create the file?
```

## Where to fix

`src/loader.js` in the `loadCategory()` function - improve the thrown error message.

issues.title: [BUG] --category flag with unknown ID shows raw JavaScript error
issues.body:
## Bug: Ugly error for unknown --category value

```bash
node index.js --category notreal
```

Currently shows a raw JS error. Should show:

```
Unknown category: "notreal"
Valid IDs: essential, themes, fun, ai, webdev, python, devtools
```

## Where to fix

`index.js` - the try/catch block for the --category flag. The list of valid IDs should be read dynamically from `data/categories/index.json`.

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add --save and --load profile flags
issues.body:
## Feature: Extension Profiles

Let users save chosen extensions as a named profile and reload later.

## Proposed usage

```bash
npx vscode-setup --save my-webdev-setup
npx vscode-setup --load my-webdev-setup
npx vscode-setup --profiles
```

Profiles saved to `~/.vscode-setup/profiles/<n>.json`

## Implementation plan

1. Add `--save`, `--load`, `--profiles` to `src/args.js`
2. After user selects extensions, write IDs to a JSON profile file if `--save` is set
3. If `--load` is set, read the file and call `installExtensions()`
4. Use `os.homedir()` + `fs.mkdirSync({ recursive: true })`

## Tests needed

- Flags parse correctly
- Profile file is created with correct content
- Loading a non-existent profile shows a helpful error

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add --search flag to find extensions by name or tag
issues.body:
## Feature: Extension Search

Let users search all extensions without going through the full menu.

## Proposed usage

```bash
npx vscode-setup --search git
npx vscode-setup --search theme
```

## Expected output

```
Results for "git" (3 found):

  1. GitLens  (eamodio.gitlens)   Essential
  2. Git Graph  (mhutchie.git-graph)   Essential

Install any? (numbers or Enter to skip):
```

## Implementation

1. Add `--search <term>` to `src/args.js`
2. Call `getAllExtensions()` from `src/loader.js`
3. Filter case-insensitively by name, description, or tags
4. Display results and optionally prompt to install

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add --update flag to reinstall extensions from extensions.json
issues.body:
## Feature: Update / Reinstall

Allow users to reinstall all extensions from an existing `.vscode/extensions.json`.

## Proposed usage

```bash
npx vscode-setup --update
npx vscode-setup --update --profile my-setup
```

## Why

Useful after a VS Code reinstall, or when someone clones a repo that has `.vscode/extensions.json`.

## Implementation

1. Add `--update` to `src/args.js`
2. Read `.vscode/extensions.json` from the current directory
3. Pass all IDs to `installExtensions()` in `src/installer.js`
4. Show a progress summary

issues.title: [FEATURE] Show extension count per category in the category picker
issues.body:
## Small UX improvement: show extension counts

When choosing a category in Step 1, show how many extensions are in each one.

## Current output

```
  1. Essential (All Devs)
     Must-haves for every developer
```

## Proposed output

```
  1. Essential (All Devs)  - 10 extensions
     Must-haves for every developer
```

## Where to fix

`src/display.js` in `printCategories()`. Each category object already has an `extensions` array with a `.length` property.

## Difficulty

Very easy. One-line change.

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add --no-color flag for plain output in CI environments
issues.body:
## Feature: Disable ANSI colors

In CI pipelines and some terminals, ANSI codes show as raw characters. Add a `--no-color` flag.

## Proposed usage

```bash
npx vscode-setup --list --no-color
```

## Implementation

1. Add `--no-color` to `src/args.js`
2. In `src/display.js`, export `setColors(enabled)` that replaces all color values with empty strings when disabled
3. Also respect the `NO_COLOR` environment variable (see no-color.org)

issues.title: [TESTS] Add tests for display.js
issues.body:
## Missing Tests: display.js

`src/display.js` handles all terminal output but has no tests.

## What to test

Create `tests/display.test.js`:

- Color constants contain ANSI escape codes (`\x1b[`)
- `printCategories()` does not throw on valid input
- `printExtensions()` does not throw on valid input
- `printSummary()` does not throw for all combinations

## Example

```js
const assert = require('assert');
const { c, printCategories } = require('../src/display');
assert.ok(c.green.includes('\x1b['));
printCategories([{ label: 'Test', description: 'desc', extensions: [] }]);
```

## Difficulty

Easy. Great starting point for new contributors.

issues.title: [TESTS] Write tests for installExtensions() with a mock
issues.body:
## Missing Tests: installExtensions()

`installExtensions()` in `src/installer.js` calls the real `code` CLI. We need to test its logic with a mock.

## What to test

- All succeed: `installed === total`, `failed === 0`
- All fail: `installed === 0`, `failed === total`
- Mixed: partial counts are correct
- `onProgress` is called once per extension
- `results` length equals input length

## Difficulty

Medium. Requires mocking/stubbing a Node.js function.

issues.title: [TESTS] Add CLI integration test for --list and --help flags
[TESTS] Add edge case tests for validateCategory()
issues.body:
## More tests for validateCategory()

`validateCategory()` in `src/loader.js` is tested but edge cases are missing.

## Edge cases to add

- Category with zero extensions (empty array) - valid or not?
- Extension with empty tags array `[]` - should be valid
- Extension with extra unknown fields - should still be valid
- Category ID with spaces - should this be caught?

## How to add

Open `tests/loader.test.js` and add `assert` blocks using the existing pattern.

## Difficulty

Easy. No new files needed.

issues.title: [REFACTOR] Split display.js into smaller focused modules
issues.body:
## Refactor: Split display.js

`src/display.js` handles colors, printing, progress, and summaries all in one file. It should be split.

## Proposed structure

```
src/display/
  colors.js       - ANSI constants
  categories.js   - printCategories, printExtensions
  progress.js     - printInstallProgress, printInstallStart
  summary.js      - printSummary, printNoCodeWarning
  banner.js       - banner(), divider()
  index.js        - re-exports everything (backward-compatible)
```

## Rules

- Keep same exports - no breaking changes
- All existing tests must still pass
- This PR should contain only this refactor - no feature additions

issues.title: [REFACTOR] Add JSDoc to all exported functions
issues.body:
## Refactor: JSDoc comments

Every exported function should have a JSDoc block. Currently they are inconsistent.

## Files to update

- `src/loader.js`
- `src/installer.js`
- `src/prompt.js`
- `src/args.js`
- `src/display.js`

## Format

```js
/**
 * Short description.
 * @param {string} id - the category id
 * @returns {object} the category object
 * @throws {Error} if the file does not exist
 */
```

## Rules

- One PR per file
- No behavior changes - documentation only

## Difficulty

Easy. Great for reading and understanding the codebase.

issues.title: [PERFORMANCE] Cache loaded categories to avoid repeated disk reads
[DOCS] Add a terminal demo GIF to the README
issues.body:
## Docs: Demo GIF

The README has a plain text demo block. A real GIF would make it much more attractive.

## What we need

A recording of:
1. Running `npx vscode-setup`
2. Picking 1-2 categories
3. Selecting extensions
4. Seeing install progress

## Free tools

- asciinema.org - records and shares as animated SVG
- terminalizer.com - exports to GIF
- github.com/charmbracelet/vhs - script your recording

## How to contribute

1. Record the demo
2. Add to `docs/assets/demo.gif`
3. Replace the text demo section in README.md with `![Demo](docs/assets/demo.gif)`
4. Open a PR

No coding required!

issues.title: [DOCS] Add a SECURITY.md file
issues.body:
## Docs: SECURITY.md

GitHub recommends every project has a SECURITY.md for reporting vulnerabilities.

## What to create

Create `SECURITY.md` at the root with:

```markdown
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

Only the latest version is actively supported.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Do NOT open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.

Instead use GitHub private vulnerability reporting:
Security tab > Report a vulnerability.

We will respond within 48 hours.
```

## How to contribute

1. Create `SECURITY.md` at the project root
2. Open a PR

Very easy. Just a Markdown file.

issues.title: [DOCS] Write a blog post or Dev.to article about this project
issues.body:
## Community: Write about vscode-setup

A blog post is one of the best ways to grow an open-source project.

## What to write about

- Why this tool exists
- How to use it (`npx vscode-setup`)
- Your favourite extensions from each category
- How to contribute

## Where to publish

- dev.to
- hashnode.com
- medium.com
- Your own blog

## After publishing

Share the link in a comment on this issue. We will add it to the README under a Community section!

issues.title: [DISCUSSION] Should we show extension download counts?
issues.body:
## Discussion: Show popularity data?

Should we show extension download counts to help users choose?

## Options

**Option A - Manual data in JSON**
Add a `downloads` field to each extension, updated manually on releases.
- Simple, no API calls, but gets stale

**Option B - Live from Marketplace API**
Fetch real-time stats from the VS Code Marketplace API.
- Always current, but requires network and adds startup time

**Option C - Do not add it**
Keep it simple. The description should sell the extension.

## Vote

React to this issue:
- +1 for Option A (manual)
- rocket for Option B (live API)
- -1 for Option C (skip it)

Comment with your reasoning!

issues.title: [DISCUSSION] Should we build a web UI for browsing extensions?
issues.body:
## Discussion: Future web UI

A web UI where you can browse, filter, and pick extensions - then get a one-click install command - could reach a much wider audience.

## What it could look like

- Browse by category with search/filter
- Check extensions you want
- Copy the install command or download `.vscode/extensions.json`
- Hosted on GitHub Pages (free)

## Options

- Plain HTML/CSS/JS reading the category JSON files directly
- Next.js or Astro for a more polished experience
- GitHub Pages for free hosting

## Questions

1. Is a web UI worth the complexity?
2. Same repo or separate one?
3. Who would build it?

Drop your thoughts!

issues.title: [DISCUSSION] What categories are missing? Vote and suggest here
issues.body:
## Open Discussion: What is Missing?

We want to grow based on what the community actually uses.

## Current categories

- Essential (All Devs)
- Themes & Appearance
- Fun & Personality
- AI & Productivity
- Web Development
- Python & Data
- Dev Tools & Utilities

## What should we add next?

Comment below with:
1. Category name + emoji
2. 2-3 must-have extensions
3. Who it is for

## Already planned

- Rust & Systems
- Go Development
- Java & Spring Boot
- Mobile (Flutter, React Native)
- C & C++
- Testing
- DevOps & Cloud
- Game Dev
- Writing & Docs
- PHP

React with +1 on comments you agree with - most upvoted get built first!
